\endgroup
\par\smallskip
\noindent\footnotesize \PAC\ model source: \texttt{UFO-SM}. Here \(n\) is final-state multiplicity. Each block reports generation seconds and wall/evaluator microseconds per point: one selected flow with the helicity sum on the left, all flows at one fixed helicity on the right. Wall measures Rusticol's public \texttt{evaluate} call; eval separately profiles the evaluator when available. Both use batch 64, stage-local inputs, ten Horner iterations, and a ten-second timing target; output chunks are 128 selected and 8192 all-flow. \AC's generated-library time is reported under wall; its separate eval metric is N/A. In this UFO-SM table, each \texttt{vs blt-in} entry is the adjacent generation- or wall-time ratio to the matching built-in-SM row at the same multiplicity, backend, and flow/helicity workload; values below one mean that UFO-SM is faster.
\end{landscape}
